proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_080 is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
Patients with feature_042=1 have higher mean pfs_months than those with feature_042=0.
Patients with feature_056=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with feature_056=0.
Patients with feature_048=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with feature_048=0.
Higher feature_063 categorical level (0 < 1 < 2) is associated with progressively lower pfs_months (monotonic dose-response).

analyses.result_summary: feature_080 (range 30-90, mean ~65) shows a strong positive linear association with pfs_months: Pearson r=0.7015, slope=+0.176 months per unit, p<1e-300. Univariate regression alone explains R^2~0.49.
Mean pfs_months: 5.408 for feature_042=1 (n=17,428) vs 4.310 for feature_042=0 (n=32,572). Difference +1.098 months, Welch t-test p<1e-300.
Mean pfs_months: 3.617 for feature_056=1 (n=14,969) vs 5.153 for feature_056=0 (n=35,031). Difference -1.536 months, p<1e-300.
Mean pfs_months: 3.750 for feature_048=1 (n=5,090) vs 4.800 for feature_048=0 (n=44,910). Difference -1.050 months, p=1.45e-178.
Mean pfs_months: level 0 = 5.640 (n=17,592), level 1 = 4.444 (n=24,971), level 2 = 3.289 (n=7,437). ANOVA F=2860.75, p<1e-300; linear trend slope = -1.180 months per level (monotonic dose-response).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_111=1 have higher mean pfs_months than those with feature_111=0.
Patients with feature_015=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with feature_015=0.
Patients with feature_040=1 have lower mean pfs_months than those with feature_040=0.
Patients with feature_039=1 have higher mean pfs_months than those with feature_039=0.

analyses.result_summary: feature_015=1 (n=17,365) mean pfs=4.328 vs feature_015=0 mean=4.887. Diff -0.559, p=6.07e-144.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_067 (continuous, range 0-24.6) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).
Higher feature_019 (continuous, range 1.5-5.5) is associated with longer pfs_months (positive linear association).
Higher feature_101 (continuous, range 1-100) is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative linear association).

analyses.result_summary: feature_067 vs pfs_months: r=-0.1155, slope=-0.0747 months per unit, p=4.07e-148. Direction confirmed (higher feature_067 -> shorter PFS).
feature_019 vs pfs_months: r=+0.1002, slope=+0.499 months per unit, p=9.39e-112. Confirmed positive linear association.
feature_101 vs pfs_months: r=-0.0914, slope=-0.0188 months per unit, p=2.98e-93. Confirmed inverse linear association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080, feature_042, feature_063, feature_056, feature_048, feature_067, feature_015, feature_111, feature_019, feature_101, and feature_040 each retain independent association with pfs_months after mutual adjustment in a single multivariable OLS model.
feature_039 and feature_034 will lose statistical significance after adjustment for the strongest 11 predictors, indicating their univariate effects are confounded.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_042 + feature_063 + feature_056 + feature_048 + feature_067 + feature_015 + feature_111 + feature_019 + feature_101 + feature_040 + feature_039 + feature_034', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable OLS R^2=0.7919 (AIC=154,888). All 11 a-priori predictors remain highly significant (p<1e-30) with adjusted coefficients close to their crude estimates: feature_080 +0.176, feature_042 +1.10, feature_063 -1.17, feature_056 -1.55, feature_048 -0.98, feature_067 -0.077, feature_015 -0.58, feature_111 +0.53, feature_019 +0.48, feature_101 -0.019, feature_040 -0.47. feature_039 (p=0.32) and feature_034 (p=0.88) lose significance after adjustment, supporting h14 (their univariate associations were confounded by the stronger predictors).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The effect of feature_042=1 on pfs_months differs across feature_063 levels (treatment-effect heterogeneity by feature_063 stratum).
The effect of feature_080 on pfs_months is attenuated in patients with feature_056=1 (negative interaction: feature_080 x feature_056).
The effects of feature_042 and feature_056 on pfs_months are additive (no interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction term feature_042:feature_063 = -0.019, p=0.367 (not significant). The +1.10-month benefit associated with feature_042=1 is consistent across feature_063 levels; h15 not supported.

Significant interaction feature_080:feature_056 = -0.0112 (p=3.1e-12). The age-like slope is +0.180/unit in feature_056=0 patients vs +0.168/unit in feature_056=1 patients - the positive feature_080 effect is meaningfully attenuated when feature_056=1.
Interaction feature_042:feature_056 = +0.006, p=0.901. Effects of feature_042 and feature_056 are essentially additive on the pfs_months scale; h17 supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Mean pfs_months differs across feature_011 (race) categories (white, black, hispanic, asian, other).
Mean pfs_months differs across feature_089 (insurance) categories (medicare, medicaid, private, uninsured).
After adjusting for feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, feature_011 (race) remains independently associated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Means by race: white 4.679 (n=32,498), black 4.717 (n=5,889), hispanic 4.707 (n=7,631), asian 4.735 (n=2,970), other 4.762 (n=1,012). ANOVA F=0.849, p=0.494. No raw association between race and pfs_months.
Means: medicaid 4.706, medicare 4.692, private 4.685, uninsured 4.733. ANOVA F=0.310, p=0.818. No raw association between insurance and pfs_months.

analyses.code: smf.ols('pfs_months ~ C(feature_011) + feature_080 + C(feature_063) + feature_056 + feature_042', df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: After adjustment, no race category coefficient is significant (all p>0.34, magnitudes ~0.02 months). Race remains null after adjustment - h20 not supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The slope of pfs_months on feature_080 is steeper among feature_063=0 patients than among feature_063=2 patients (interaction feature_080 x feature_063).
The age-like slope of pfs_months on feature_080 differs by feature_042 status (interaction feature_080 x feature_042).
The age-like slope of pfs_months on feature_080 is attenuated in feature_048=1 patients (interaction feature_080 x feature_048 < 0).

analyses.result_summary: feature_080:C(feature_063)[T.2] = -0.0129, p=4.1e-09 (significant). Slope is +0.179/unit at level 0, +0.176/unit at level 1, +0.166/unit at level 2 - small but monotone attenuation as feature_063 worsens.
feature_080:feature_042 = -0.0032, p=0.044 (borderline). Slope very slightly lower (+0.174 vs +0.177) for feature_042=1; magnitude small relative to main effects.
feature_080:feature_048 = -0.0076, p=0.004. Confirmed: feature_080 effect on PFS is weaker when feature_048=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The relationship between feature_080 and pfs_months has a positive quadratic component (curvature, not strictly linear).
feature_019 shows a monotonic dose-response across binned levels (no plateau or non-monotonicity).
feature_067 shows a monotonic dose-response across binned levels.

analyses.result_summary: Quadratic coefficient = +0.000604, p=6.5e-25 (positive curvature). Mean PFS by age bin: 30-50=1.43, 50-60=3.06, 60-70=4.67, 70-80=6.32, 80-90=8.20 - increments grow slightly with age, consistent with positive curvature.
Mean pfs by feature_019 bin: 1.5-3 = 4.20, 3-3.5 = 4.45, 3.5-4 = 4.72, 4-4.5 = 4.90, 4.5-5.5 = 5.16. Monotone increasing across all bins; h25 supported.
Mean pfs by feature_067 bin: 0-1 = 4.97, 1-3 = 4.78, 3-5 = 4.70, 5-10 = 4.48, 10-30 = 4.00. Monotone decreasing; h26 supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within each feature_056 stratum, all 7 of (feature_080, feature_042, feature_063, feature_048, feature_111, feature_015) retain the same sign and statistical significance, indicating consistent direction of effect across baseline-risk groups.

There is a positive (antagonistic) interaction between feature_056 and feature_063: among feature_056=1 patients, the per-level decrement of feature_063 on pfs_months is smaller than among feature_056=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: Stratum feature_056=0 (n=35,031): all six covariates significant, signs match overall model (feature_080 +0.179, feature_042 +1.09, feature_063 level 1 -1.20, level 2 -2.36, feature_048 -0.98, feature_111 +0.54, feature_015 -0.59). Stratum feature_056=1 (n=14,969): same signs, similar magnitudes (feature_080 +0.168, feature_042 +1.11, feature_063 level 1 -1.15, level 2 -2.26, feature_048 -0.98, feature_111 +0.56, feature_015 -0.59). Effects are consistent across both strata; h27 supported.

feature_056:feature_063 interaction = +0.0611, p=0.003. Per-level decrement of feature_063 is slightly smaller in absolute value among feature_056=1 patients - consistent with mild floor effect. Direction confirmed (positive antagonistic) but magnitude is small relative to main effects (~5% attenuation).

proposed_hypotheses.text: A composite multivariable OLS model with the 11 strongest features (feature_080, feature_042, feature_063, feature_056, feature_048, feature_067, feature_015, feature_111, feature_019, feature_101, feature_040) explains >70% of pfs_months variance.
After adjusting for those 11 features, residual variation in pfs_months remains modestly correlated with feature_053 (negative direction), indicating a small additional independent association.

analyses.result_summary: Composite OLS achieves R^2 = 0.7919 (adj 0.7919). All 11 features retain p<1e-50 in the multivariable model. h29 supported.

feature_053 vs residual r=-0.0368 (effect on pfs_months ~-0.037 per unit of standardized feature_053), p=1.87e-16. Significant but small additional independent inverse association. The next-largest residual signal (feature_126 binary, effect on residual -0.176, p=0.002) and feature_037 (p=0.012) are weaker. Overall, residual structure is modest, indicating the 11-feature model captures the bulk of explainable variance.
